title: Transform Prayer
description: Convert active sentences into passive sentences and vice versa.

sentences.prompt: Activa → Pasiva: "The chef cooks the meal."
sentences.correct: The meal is cooked by the chef

sentences.prompt: Activa → Pasiva: "They built the bridge in 1950."
Pasiva → Activa: "The book was written by J.K. Rowling."
Activa → Pasiva: "They have sent the email."
Pasiva → Activa: "The cake was made by my mother."
Activa → Pasiva: "They will complete the project."
Activa → Pasiva: "You can fix this problem."
Activa → Pasiva: "They teach Spanish in this school."
sentences.correct: Spanish is taught in this school

sentences.prompt: Pasiva → Activa: "The window was broken by the children."
Activa → Pasiva: "Leonardo painted the Mona Lisa."
sentences.correct: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo

title: Activa, Pasiva o Ambas
description: Choose the correct form according to the context. When is passive better?

questions.question: The email ___ yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past passive: "The email was sent yesterday."

questions.question: The bridge ___ ___ (build) in 1920.
questions.explanation: Past passive: "The bridge was built in 1920."

questions.question: In a science report, which is more appropriate?
questions.explanation: Scientific writing prefers passive voice: "The experiment was conducted..."

questions.question: English ___ in many countries around the world.
questions.explanation: Present passive for general facts: "English is spoken in many countries."

questions.question: The project will ___ ___ (complete) by next Friday.
My wallet ___! I can't find it anywhere.
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: "My wallet has been stolen."

questions.question: "Hamlet" ___ by Shakespeare.
questions.explanation: Past passive with "by" agent: "Hamlet was written by Shakespeare."

questions.question: The new hospital ___ last year.
questions.explanation: Past passive: "The new hospital was opened last year."

questions.question: Which sentence uses passive voice correctly?
options: The cake was baked by Maria.
The cake has baking by Maria.
The cake is bake by Maria.
questions.explanation: Correct passive: Object + was + past participle + by agent.

title: Tiempo Verbal → Forma Pasiva
description: Match each verb tense with its passive voice structure.

title: Transformaciones Activa ↔ Pasiva
description: Practice mental transformation before flipping the card.

cards.example: "By people" is omitted because it's obvious.

cards.back: Passive: Hamlet was written by Shakespeare.
cards.example: "By Shakespeare" is included because the agent matters.

cards.front: Active: They have sent the invitation.
cards.back: Passive: The invitation has been sent.
cards.example: Present perfect passive: has/have been + past participle.
"By someone" is omitted because the agent is unknown.

cards.front: Active: They will finish the project tomorrow.
cards.back: Passive: The project will be finished tomorrow.
cards.example: Future passive: will be + past participle.

cards.front: Active: You must wear a helmet.
cards.back: Passive: A helmet must be worn.
cards.example: Modal passive: modal + be + past participle.

cards.back: Passive: Cars are manufactured by the company.
cards.example: Present simple passive: am/is/are + past participle.

cards.front: Active: They are building a new hospital.
cards.back: Passive: A new hospital is being built.
cards.example: Present continuous passive: am/is/are being + past participle.

cards.back: Passive: America was discovered by Columbus.

cards.front: Active: They grow rice in Asia.
cards.back: Passive: Rice is grown in Asia.
cards.example: "By them" omitted — the focus is on rice, not the farmers.

cards.front: Active: A famous architect designed this building.
cards.back: Passive: This building was designed by a famous architect.
cards.example: The agent is included because it adds important information.

cards.front: Active: You can download the app for free.
cards.back: Passive: The app can be downloaded for free.

cards.front: Active: They have not found the solution yet.
cards.back: Passive: The solution has not been found yet.
Passive: The experiment was conducted by scientists.
cards.example: Common in academic and scientific writing.

title: The Interactive Newspaper
description: Explore the newspaper headlines and discover the passive voice in the news.

items.label: THE DAILY NEWS
items.labelEn: The Daily News — A newspaper full of passive voice

items.label: 📰 Scientific award
items.labelEn: Award Was Given to Young Scientist

items.label: 📰 Pintura encontrada
items.labelEn: Lost Painting Has Been Found in Attic

items.label: 📰 Ley aprobada
items.labelEn: New Law Will Be Approved Next Month

items.label: 📰 Hospital construido
items.labelEn: Hospital Was Built in Record Time

items.label: 📰 Thief caught
items.labelEn: Thief Was Caught by Security Cameras

items.label: 📰 Vacuna desarrollada
items.labelEn: New Vaccine Has Been Developed by International Team

items.label: ✏️ Editorial
items.labelEn: Passive voice is used in news because the event matters more than the agent

title: Voz Pasiva
description: Decipher terms of the passive voice.

title: Activa, Pasiva o Ambas Listening
description: Listen and choose the correct answer.

items.question: What did you hear?
options: The meal is cooked by the chef
items.explanation: You heard: "The meal is cooked by the chef".

items.question: What did you hear?
options: The meal is cooked by the chef
items.explanation: You heard: "The bridge was built in 1950".

items.question: What did you hear?
options: The meal is cooked by the chef
items.explanation: You heard: "J.K. Rowling wrote the book".
